reason: Response B demonstrates the stronger creative craft typical of the more capable Sonnet tier: a named character (Gerald), a sustained comedic voice, specific running gags (the dog with legs, the plastic dinosaur from February, the "low-light plant" reframe), and a callback-laden structure with a memorable sign-off. Response A is competent and well-organized but more formulaic and generic (numbered grievances, stock complaints about water/light/dust/temperature), which reads as the smaller Haiku tier. The richer specificity, characterization, and comedic timing in B point to Sonnet.
